creator: kristinacors
caption: Hot take: You don’t need a fancy camera to be a travel creator. You honestly just need your iPhone! #travelcontentcreator #travelcore #smallcontentcreators #bestphoneforcontentcreators #shotoniphone

transcription.text: Answering the questions I always get asked as a travel content creator. People always ask, how do you get shots like this and this? As a travel content creator with over 800,000 followers across platforms, I could tell you, you do not need fancy equipment to get started. I strictly use my iPhone 16 Pro Max and the camera is super crisp. And honestly, I've tried the professional cameras and here is my honest opinion about them. I just feel like they're bulky and heavy to bring on trips, so I always feel like my arm is going to fall off whenever I'm using them. Also, because they're expensive, I'm always worried about them getting lost or stolen or damaged by water or by sand or dropped on a hike. And then to me, transferring those memory cards is so annoying, I swear. It just demotivates me from actually posting content. I just feel like it's so much easier to pull up my iPhone in the moment and use it. Plus, the iPhone is much more lightweight, so I don't feel like I'm getting carpal tunnel by the end of my trip. And in general, I'm a lot less anxious about it being lost or stolen or damaged because I always have it on me. So if you're wondering if you need that fancy equipment to get started as a travel content creator, you don't. Let this be your sign. You can get that crisp, quality content you're looking for by shooting on your iPhone. By the way, if you're a travel content creator, I'm curious to hear from you about whether you use a professional camera or your iPhone to shoot content. Let me know.
